title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed Any Lucia Lopez Belloza's lawsuit after she declined a return flight to the U.S.
Lopez Belloza, a Babson student, was deported to Honduras despite a court order intending to keep her in the U.S.
Her lawyer claims ICE tactics hinder legal processes; the court found no concealment efforts by the government.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson calls Derrick White a top-5 player based on advanced analytics.
Derrick White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists per game this season for the Boston Celtics.
White plays a key defensive role, especially impactful with Jayson Tatum's absence early in the season.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scores 23 points, leading Celtics to 109-98 win over Cavaliers in Cleveland.
Jayson Tatum returns strong from injury, scoring 20 points despite shooting struggles.
Celtics' defense holds Cavaliers to 10 second-quarter points, showcasing an elite performance.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advises against a large contract for Alec Pierce due to limited route versatility.
Alec Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025 with 47 catches for 1,003 yards last year.
The Patriots, with over $50 million cap space, seek new targets after releasing Stefon Diggs this offseason.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown police officer Brittany Dougal died by suicide after battling mental illness, as announced by the department.
Dougal, 29, was newly sworn-in and graduated second in her class from Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy.
Dougal's funeral will be held in Danvers, MA; family requests donations to North Kingstown Animal Shelter or Tunnel to Towers Foundation.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are considering under-the-radar free agents, including Vikings WR Jalen Nailor and Giants TE Daniel Bellinger.
Potential free agent targets for the Patriots include Edge rushers Joseph Ossai, Arden Key, Kwity Paye, and Al-Quadin Muhammad.
With a $60 million cap space, Patriots might add players like Devin Bush or explore signing versatile O-lineman like Teven Jenkins.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, a Raiders minority owner, reportedly blocked a Maxx Crosby trade to the Patriots, according to ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.
The Raiders traded Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall in the 2026 draft.
Patriots may seek free-agency options to strengthen their edge rusher position, linking to Jaelan Phillips and Boye Mafe.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Colts did not place franchise tag on wide receiver Alec Pierce, making him likely to enter free agency.
Alec Pierce is expected to receive offers of at least $27 million per year, possibly staying with the Colts.
Patriots are interested in acquiring a wide receiver, considering options like Alec Pierce after releasing Stefon Diggs.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, 30, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Boston Public Library.
Flynn struggled with dyslexia and ADHD, finding sanctuary and support in libraries throughout her academic journey.
Despite a rare vascular condition and four leg surgeries, Flynn is determined to complete the marathon, emphasizing persistence.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, will run her 13th Boston Marathon in April 2026.
Comander is dedicating her run to a patient, Christina, who is undergoing breast cancer treatment.
The marathon run supports the Ellie Fund, aiding breast cancer patients with daily challenges.
